title: Competitor Scan Report
description: A competitor scan report produced by engine/orchestrator/competitor-scan.js and stored at $CONTENT_HOME/scans/<brand>/<YYYY-MM-DD>.json (Zone U, retention_class:transient — release-spec roadmap #5). PATTERNS ONLY: no field in this schema may carry verbatim competitor text — only counts, ratios, and label strings (P1 guarantee). Always Zone U (untrusted external competitive signals). The freshness_window feeds the DD-15 stale guard used by the voice-calibration propose step.

period.description: The reporting period this scan covers (the window the competitor corpus was captured in).

start.description: Start of the observation period (ISO 8601 date-time).

end.description: End of the observation period (ISO 8601 date-time).

brand.description: Brand id this scan was run for (matches brand.json id).

platform.description: Platform descriptor id the competitor corpus was observed on (e.g. 'twitter', 'instagram'). Use 'mixed' when items span platforms.

drama_markers.description: Aggregated DRAMA SIGNAL counts across the competitor corpus (counts and ratios only — NO verbatim text). Measures the frequency of dramatic/hype textual patterns.

high_drama_count.description: Items classified as high-drama (hype terms, exclamation, scarcity language, ALL CAPS segments).

medium_drama_count.description: Items classified as medium-drama (moderate urgency, some exclamation).

low_drama_count.description: Items classified as low-drama (factual, calm tone).

exclamation_rate.description: Fraction (0..1) of competitor items containing one or more exclamation marks.

hype_term_rate.description: Fraction (0..1) of competitor items matching hype-term patterns (e.g. 'biggest', 'HUGE', 'don't miss', 'last chance').

archetype_distribution.description: Per-archetype code counts across own vs competitor corpus items. LABELS only (archetype code strings) — NO verbatim text. Each entry is one archetype code with own-corpus count and competitor-corpus count.

code.description: Archetype code from the engine archetype taxonomy (e.g. HOW_TO, TEASER, SKY_TONIGHT). Labels only — never verbatim competitor text.

own_count.description: Number of own-corpus items assigned this archetype code.

competitor_count.description: Number of competitor-corpus items assigned this archetype code.

hook_signals.description: Aggregated HOOK / opening-pattern signals across the competitor corpus (pattern labels and counts only — NO verbatim competitor text).

total_items.description: Number of competitor corpus items with a detectable opening-pattern hook.

top_patterns.description: Top detected opening-hook patterns, sorted by count descending. Pattern LABELS only (e.g. 'how-to-numbered', 'question-hook', 'announcement-breaking') — NEVER verbatim competitor text.

pattern.description: A label for the detected hook pattern (e.g. 'how-to-numbered', 'question-hook', 'direct-tip', 'announcement-breaking', 'numbered-list'). Label strings only — never competitor copy.

count.description: Number of competitor corpus items matching this hook pattern.

cadence_profile.description: Posting cadence and structural preferences observed across the competitor corpus (counts and ratios only).

total_items.description: Total corpus items used for cadence profiling.

avg_posts_per_week.description: Average competitor posts per week across the observation period.

thread_rate.description: Fraction (0..1) of competitor items that are multi-part / thread format.

media_rate.description: Fraction (0..1) of competitor items containing media (images, video, GIFs).

top_days.description: Day-of-week labels (mon–sun) sorted by competitor posting frequency descending (inferred from captured_at timestamps).

engagement_profile.description: Engagement metric aggregates across the competitor corpus (counts and medians only — NO verbatim text, NO attribution to individual competitors beyond the distribution).

metric.description: Primary engagement metric used for ranking (e.g. 'bookmarks', 'likes', 'reposts').

median_value.description: Median value of the primary engagement metric across all competitor corpus items with metrics.

high_engagement_archetype_codes.description: Archetype codes (labels only) that appear disproportionately often among the top-performing competitor corpus items by the primary metric.

drama_signal.description: Aggregate drama signal for the competitor landscape: low, medium, or high. Derived deterministically from drama_markers counts (high when hype_term_rate > 0.4 or exclamation_rate > 0.5; medium 0.15–0.4; low below).

confidence.description: Confidence level (0..1) of the scan analysis — a function of corpus size and item coverage. Below a minimum threshold, the voice-calibration propose step degrades to a low-confidence warning.

freshness_window.description: How long this scan report remains actionable. Consumed by the DD-15 stale guard in the voice-calibration propose step (a report older than competitor_scan.voice_calibration.freshness_days must NOT advance to a proposal).

duration.description: Duration the scan stays fresh, as an ISO 8601 duration (e.g. P30D, P7D).

expires_at.description: Absolute time the scan freshness window closes.

provenance.description: How this report was produced. Always Zone U (untrusted external competitive signals), whether submitted via manual/export or adapter-fetched.

trust_zone.description: Trust zone. Always U for competitor scan reports (untrusted external input, RD-8).

method.description: How the report was produced: manual/export corpus submission or a registered adapter.

submitted_at.description: When the report was produced / the scan completed.

corpus_item_count.description: Total corpus items the scan analyzed (own + competitor).
